analysis.consistency: The feature is consistent with the core Barbarian class progression (gained at level 2). The mechanics described align with established D&D 5e terminology.
analysis.quality: High quality. The description is clear, precise, and uses standard game terminology. The prerequisites ([blinded], [deafened], [incapacitated]) are standard limitations for perception-based advantages.

mechanics.effect: Advantage on Dexterity saving throws against visible effects (traps, spells).
limitations: Must be able to see the effect.
mechanics.assessment: Mechanically sound. Provides excellent defensive utility against common threats (Dex saves) provided the Barbarian has their senses intact.

analysis.notes: This feature scales well with the Barbarian's natural inclination towards high Dexterity scores and resistance to common disabling conditions (though Rage does not prevent being Incapacitated or Blinded, these conditions do stop the Danger Sense benefit).
